question_text: When you compress a gas into a smaller space, what happens to its pressure and why?
correct_answer: The pressure increases because the particles are forced closer together and collide with the container walls more frequently
explanation: When a gas is compressed, its particles are squeezed into a smaller volume. This means more particles hit the same area of wall in the same time, so the pressure increases. This is why a bicycle pump gets harder to push as you compress the air inside.
